name: data-pipeline
label: Data Pipeline
description: Data engineering, ETL, analytics, or workflow orchestration project.
profile_description: Pipelines run on remote infrastructure. Local runs for development and smoke testing only.
run_context_additions: - Local runs are for development and smoke testing only — not production scale.
- Record for every production run: trigger, input dataset version, output location, row counts, runtime.
- Cost acknowledgment required before any large-scale remote execution.
- Treat data mutations as irreversible — prefer append-only or versioned writes.
- Always test with a small sample before running at full scale.
workflow_notes: Design every pipeline step for idempotency — assume it may run more than once.
Log input and output record counts for every pipeline run in `docs/PROGRESS.md`.
Use the `bounded-loop-runner` skill for backfill jobs or batch iterations.
Test with a small data sample before running at full scale.
Record pipeline run metadata (trigger, duration, row counts) in the action log.
safety_notes: Data mutations may be irreversible — verify inputs and outputs before any destructive step.
Cost acknowledgment required before large-scale remote execution.
Keep credentials, connection strings, and API keys out of code and version control.
Never run a full-scale pipeline without a passing smoke test on sample data.
